title: Cox's Bazar Tour
heading: The World's Longest Beach: My Cox's Bazar Diary
subtitle: Life
author: Md Shakil Hossen

metadata.description: A personal travel diary from Cox's Bazar — sunsets over the Bay of Bengal, fresh seafood, and long walks on the world's longest natural sea beach.

contents.title: Trip Highlights
contents.content:
• Watched a stunning sunset from Himchori beach.
  • Explored the coral island of Saint Martin's by speedboat.
  • Savoured fresh grilled seafood at the Cox's Bazar market.

contents.title: Arrival at the World's Longest Beach
contents.content: Cox's Bazar is Bangladesh's crown jewel of natural tourism — a 120-kilometre unbroken stretch of sandy beach hugging the Bay of Bengal. When you first step onto that beach and see the ocean stretching to the horizon in both directions, it genuinely takes your breath away. The salt air, the roar of the waves, and the endless blue sky — it's impossible not to feel immediately lighter.

contents.title: Himchori: Waterfalls Meets the Sea
contents.content: A short ride south of Cox's Bazar town lies Himchori — a dramatic hillside where a small waterfall cascades down to meet the beach. We climbed to the top of the ridge at golden hour and watched the sun dissolve into the Bay of Bengal in a wash of orange and pink. It was one of those moments you instinctively try to photograph but know no image will ever do justice to.

contents.title: Saint Martin's: The Coral Island
contents.content: A day-trip by speedboat took us to Saint Martin's Island — Bangladesh's only coral island and a diver's paradise. The water here is a translucent turquoise, shallow enough to walk out and see starfish and coral formations beneath your feet. We snorkelled in the morning and spent the afternoon exploring the island on foot, ending with a massive plate of grilled hilsa and prawn at a local restaurant by the shore.

contents.title: Reflections
contents.content: Trips like Cox's Bazar remind me of why disconnecting periodically is not a luxury but a necessity. The best ideas I've had — the clearest thinking — often come after stepping away from the screen and letting the mind breathe. I came back from this trip recharged, with fresh perspective and renewed energy for everything I'm building.
